edges.target: Aberrant calcium signaling in growth plate chondrocytes

edges.source: Aberrant calcium signaling in growth plate chondrocytes

edges.target: Disrupted TGF-beta signaling in skeletal development

edges.source: Disrupted TGF-beta signaling in skeletal development

nodes.description: Constitutively active TRPV4 channels in growth plate chondrocytes lead to aberrant calcium influx. TRPV4 is selectively expressed in cartilage, and the gain-of-function mutations perturb calcium homeostasis in the cells responsible for endochondral ossification of the vertebral column.
nodes.id: Aberrant calcium signaling in growth plate chondrocytes

evidence.reference_title: Gain-of-function mutations in TRPV4 cause autosomal dominant brachyolmia.
evidence.snippet: we selected TRPV4, which encodes a calcium permeable cation channel of the transient receptor potential (TRP) vanilloid family, as a candidate gene because of its cartilage-selective gene expression pattern

evidence.reference_title: TRPV4 expresses in bone cell lineages and TRPV4-R616Q mutant causing Brachyolmia in human reveals 'loss-of-interaction' with cholesterol.
evidence.snippet: TRPV4 is expressed in Mesenchymal stem cells and the localization of TRPV4 in lipid raft is dependent on temperature and cholesterol

nodes.description: Deficient PAPS supply impairs sulfation of chondroitin sulfate proteoglycans in cartilage extracellular matrix. Undersulfated proteoglycans compromise the structural integrity of growth plate cartilage and disrupt Indian hedgehog (Ihh) signaling. Ihh normally binds to chondroitin sulfate chains on aggrecan; in undersulfated cartilage, Ihh distribution is abnormal and hedgehog signaling is diminished, leading to significantly reduced chondrocyte proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and radiographic features of the autosomal recessive form of brachyolmia caused by PAPSS2 mutations.
evidence.snippet: PAPSS2-brachyolmia includes phenotypes of the conventional clinical concept of brachyolmia, the Hobaek and Toledo types, and is associated with abnormal androgen metabolism

evidence.reference_title: Sulfation of chondroitin sulfate proteoglycans is necessary for proper Indian hedgehog signaling in the developing growth plate.
evidence.snippet: bm limb growth plates showed diminished Indian hedgehog (Ihh) signaling and abnormal Ihh protein distribution in the extracellular matrix

evidence.reference_title: Sulfation of chondroitin sulfate proteoglycans is necessary for proper Indian hedgehog signaling in the developing growth plate.
evidence.snippet: BrdU incorporation exhibited a significant reduction in chondrocyte proliferation

nodes.description: Loss-of-function mutations in PAPSS2 impair the biosynthesis of PAPS (3'-phosphoadenosine 5'-phosphosulfate), the universal sulfate donor required for all sulfation reactions. In vitro enzyme assays confirm that both frameshift and missense PAPSS2 mutations result in loss of enzymatic function.

evidence.reference_title: PAPSS2 mutations cause autosomal recessive brachyolmia.
evidence.snippet: identified its disease gene, PAPSS2 (phosphoadenosine-phosphosulfate synthetase 2)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and radiographic features of the autosomal recessive form of brachyolmia caused by PAPSS2 mutations.
evidence.snippet: In vitro enzyme assays showed that the missense mutations were also loss-of-function mutations.

nodes.description: Loss of LTBP3 function in ameloblasts impairs enamel matrix protein synthesis and amelogenesis, resulting in hypoplastic amelogenesis imperfecta with thin to absent enamel.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the latent TGF-beta binding protein 3 (LTBP3) gene cause brachyolmia with amelogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: an identical phenotype, characterized by significant short stature with brachyolmia and hypoplastic amelogenesis imperfecta (AI) with almost absent enamel

nodes.description: Loss of LTBP3 function impairs TGF-beta signaling in growth plate chondrocytes, disrupting endochondral ossification and leading to platyspondyly.
nodes.id: Disrupted TGF-beta signaling in skeletal development

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the latent TGF-beta binding protein 3 (LTBP3) gene cause brachyolmia with amelogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: an identical phenotype, characterized by significant short stature with brachyolmia and hypoplastic amelogenesis imperfecta (AI) with almost absent enamel

nodes.description: PAPSS2 deficiency also disrupts sulfation of dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) to DHEAS, leading to low serum DHEAS. This is a biochemically distinct consequence of the same upstream PAPS deficiency, but overt androgen excess is not observed.

evidence.reference_title: PAPSS2-related brachyolmia: Clinical and radiological phenotype in 18 new cases.
evidence.snippet: Low serum DHEAS, but not overt androgen excess was identified.

nodes.description: All three brachyolmia pathways converge on impaired endochondral ossification in the vertebral growth plates. This results in generalized platyspondyly — the hallmark radiographic finding shared across all subtypes. The predominantly axial skeletal involvement distinguishes brachyolmia from more generalized spondyloepiphyseal or spondylometaphyseal dysplasias.

evidence.reference_title: PAPSS2 mutations cause autosomal recessive brachyolmia.
evidence.snippet: Brachyolmia is a heterogeneous group of skeletal dysplasias that primarily affects the spine.

nodes.description: Recessive mutations in LTBP3 disrupt latent TGF-beta binding protein 3, which sequesters and presents latent TGF-beta for activation. LTBP3 is expressed in developing bone undergoing endochondral ossification and in differentiated ameloblasts during tooth formation.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the latent TGF-beta binding protein 3 (LTBP3) gene cause brachyolmia with amelogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: recessive hypomorphic mutations including deletion, nonsense and splice mutations, in the LTBP3 gene, which is involved in the TGF-beta signaling pathway

nodes.description: Gain-of-function mutations in TRPV4 (R616Q, V620I) cause constitutive activation of a calcium-permeable mechanosensitive cation channel with cartilage-selective expression. The R616Q mutation disrupts a cholesterol-binding motif, leading to loss of lipid raft localization. Both mutations increase constitutive channel activity and elevate responses to mechanical stimulation and agonist stimulation.

evidence.reference_title: Gain-of-function mutations in TRPV4 cause autosomal dominant brachyolmia.
evidence.snippet: both mutations resulted in a dramatic gain of function characterized by increased constitutive activity

evidence.reference_title: Wild-type and brachyolmia-causing mutant TRPV4 channels respond directly to stretch force.
evidence.snippet: R616Q channels maintain mechanosensitivity but have greater constitutive activity and no change in unitary conductance or rectification

evidence.reference_title: TRPV4 expresses in bone cell lineages and TRPV4-R616Q mutant causing Brachyolmia in human reveals 'loss-of-interaction' with cholesterol.
evidence.snippet: TRPV4-Wt but not the TRPV4-R616Q localizes preferably in cholesterol-enriched lipid rafts in osteogenic cell line Saos2
